data.name: checkBoxUseEOS.ToolTip
data.value: Enable the equation of state (EOS): compute the pressure from the current unit-cell volume using the parameters set below.

data.name: numericBox3rdVinetBeta.ToolTip
data.value: Empirical parameter β of the 3rd-order Vinet equation of state (used only when '3rd Vinet' is selected).

data.name: numericBox3rdVinetIta.ToolTip
data.value: Empirical parameter η of the 3rd-order Vinet equation of state (used only when '3rd Vinet' is selected).

data.name: numericBox3rdVinetPsi.ToolTip
data.value: Empirical parameter ψ of the 3rd-order Vinet equation of state (used only when '3rd Vinet' is selected).

data.name: numericBoxEOS_A.ToolTip
data.value: Thermal-expansion coefficient a (in 10⁻⁵/K) in α(T)=a+bT+c/T², used to expand the reference volume with temperature.

data.name: numericBoxEOS_B.ToolTip
data.value: Thermal-expansion coefficient b (in 10⁻⁹/K²), the temperature-linear term of α(T)=a+bT+c/T².

data.name: numericBoxEOS_C.ToolTip
data.value: Thermal-expansion coefficient c (in K), the c/T² term of α(T)=a+bT+c/T² (low-temperature correction).

data.name: numericBoxEOS_Gamma0.ToolTip
data.value: Grüneisen parameter γ₀ at the reference volume (dimensionless) — measures how strongly thermal vibrational energy contributes to pressure; used by the Mie–Grüneisen model.

data.name: numericBoxEOS_K0.ToolTip
data.value: Isothermal bulk modulus K₀ at the reference temperature T₀, in GPa — the resistance of the material to uniform compression (larger = stiffer).

data.name: numericBoxEOS_Kp0.ToolTip
data.value: First pressure derivative of the bulk modulus, K'₀ = (∂K/∂P) at P=0 (dimensionless, typically ≈4) — how rapidly the material stiffens under pressure.

data.name: numericBoxEOS_KpInfinity.ToolTip
numericBoxEOS_KperT.ToolTip
data.value: Temperature derivative of the bulk modulus, ∂K/∂T at P=0 (in GPa/K) — how much the stiffness K changes per kelvin (usually negative).

data.name: numericBoxEOS_Kpp0.ToolTip
data.value: Second pressure derivative of the bulk modulus, K''₀ = (∂²K/∂P²) at P=0, in 1/GPa; used by the 4th-order Birch–Murnaghan equation of state.

data.name: numericBoxEOS_Q.ToolTip
data.value: Volume-dependence exponent q of the Grüneisen parameter, γ = γ₀·(V/V₀)^q (dimensionless).

data.name: numericBoxEOS_T0.ToolTip
data.value: Reference temperature T₀ (in K) at which the isothermal parameters V₀, K₀, K'₀ are defined (typically 300 K).

data.name: numericBoxEOS_Theta0.ToolTip
numericBoxEOS_V0perCell.ToolTip
data.value: Reference unit-cell volume V₀ at T₀ and P=0, in Å³ per cell. Click to edit this field (the cm³/mol field is then derived automatically).

data.name: numericBoxEOS_V0perMol.ToolTip
data.value: Reference molar volume V₀ at T₀ and P=0, in cm³/mol. Click to edit this field (the Å³/cell field is then derived automatically).

data.name: numericBoxPressure.ToolTip
data.value: Calculated pressure P (in GPa), derived by the equation of state from the current unit-cell volume and temperature (read-only output).

data.name: numericBoxTemperature.ToolTip
data.value: Target temperature T (in K) at which the pressure is evaluated; the thermal pressure is added from the difference T − T₀.

data.name: radioButtonEOS_AP2.ToolTip
data.value: Use the AP2 (2nd-order adapted polynomial / Holzapfel) equation of state for the isothermal pressure–volume relation.

data.name: radioButtonEOS_FourthBirchMunaghan.ToolTip
data.value: Use the 4th-order Birch–Murnaghan (BM) equation of state for the isothermal pressure–volume relation (adds the K''₀ term).

data.name: radioButtonEOS_Keane.ToolTip
data.value: Use the Keane equation of state for the isothermal pressure–volume relation (uses the high-pressure limit K'∞).

data.name: radioButtonEOS_ThirdBirchMurnaghan.ToolTip
data.value: Use the 3rd-order Birch–Murnaghan (BM) equation of state for the isothermal pressure–volume relation (the standard choice; uses V₀, K₀, K'₀).

data.name: radioButtonEOS_Vinet.ToolTip
data.value: Use the Vinet equation of state for the isothermal pressure–volume relation (often preferred at strong compression; uses V₀, K₀, K'₀).

data.name: radioButtonEOS_Vinet3rd.ToolTip
data.value: Use the extended 3rd-order Vinet equation of state for the isothermal pressure–volume relation (uses the extra parameters η, β, ψ).

data.name: radioButtonMieGruneisen.ToolTip
data.value: Compute the thermal pressure from lattice vibrations using the Mie–Grüneisen model (with parameters γ₀, Debye temperature θ₀ and q).

data.name: radioButtonTdependenceK0andV0.ToolTip
data.value: Account for temperature by making the reference volume V₀ and bulk modulus K₀ temperature-dependent (uses thermal-expansion a,b,c and ∂K/∂T).

data.name: textBoxEOS_Note.ToolTip
data.value: Free-text note for this equation-of-state parameter set (e.g. literature source or comment).

data.name: label83.ToolTip
data.value: Free-text note for this equation-of-state parameter set (e.g. literature source or comment).
